mutual.label: THE DOOR IN · WHO GOVERNS
mutual.title: The Library Mutual
mutual.summary: A cooperative whose members are libraries and the people they serve — the body that governs and votes. It holds in common the one thing worth holding: the accountable authority to connect facts into conclusions.
points: Members: public & academic libraries, standards bodies, research institutions — and their patrons
Publishers and big tech get a seat at the table, never a vote on the rules
Vote-weighting between metro systems and small districts is still open
Inclusion is by values alignment to the public-library ethos

links.label: Mutual Capital Company — the legal vessel

commons.label: WHAT IS HELD
commons.title: The Meaning Commons
commons.summary: The shared, un-fenced body of trustworthy knowledge the Mutual exists to protect and tend. The asset worth holding isn't the facts — those can be open to all — but the authority to make connections.
points: Facts are open; the governed asset is the accountable connection
A healthy Commons steadily retires disagreements through real work
Findings are added back as first-class knowledge objects

miso.label: HOW IT WORKS
miso.title: MISO — Multidimensional Intelligent Semantic Object
miso.summary: The underlying technology — mostly invisible, like the plumbing protocol behind a tap. Every MISO is secure by design, already post-quantum cryptographically secure, carrying its own provenance and value-flow.
points: Carries provenance, credibility, history, and value-flow within itself
A Finding is itself a first-class MISO
Secure by design — post-quantum from the start

guild.label: WHO WEAVES THE MEANING
guild.title: The Wisdom Weaving Guild
guild.summary: A new civic profession — practitioners who compose Findings by weaving threads of fact into defensible, contestable narratives. Where the machine lays down only the threads it can account for, the Weaver composes the tapestry they make. The Guild holds the human judgment that machines structurally cannot — and is portable enough to seed in university curricula or any knowledge commons, even where the Mutual or MISOs have not yet arrived.
points: Three ranked-equal roles in tensegrity: Postulant, Journeyer, Elder
Membership earned by demonstrated alignment with the Guild's ethos, not credentials
Publishes Findings into the Meaning Commons as first-class MISOs
Inherits the library custodial tradition; honors Aunty Dr Mary Graham AM's principle that knowing carries an obligation of care
A tapestry shows its structure where a generated image hides its seams

ethos.label: A GROUNDING ETHOS
ethos.title: Knowledge offered as gift, held in interdependence
ethos.summary: Before there was economy, there was gift. The Earth's conditions for life were given, not earned — and every act of knowledge rests on that first offering. Contribution to the Commons is understood first as a gift; attribution and reciprocity exist to honor it, not to motivate it.
points: Acknowledgement of Country: offered from Bundjalung Country, land of the Goodjingburra clan
Aunty Dr Mary Graham AM's two axioms: the Land is the Law, and You Are Not Alone in the World
Pratityasamutpada — interdependent co-arising: nothing arises independently
Set within Joanna Macy's Great Turning, with compassion as the operative ethic
Indigenous knowledge carried forward only on its custodians' terms — consent living, attribution non-severable

civ.label: DEFINES WHAT THE MUTUAL DOES
civ.title: The Civilizing Role
civ.summary: The founding manifesto — six concrete roles libraries are uniquely positioned to play in the age of AI. These define much of what the Library Mutual actually does in the world.
points: Libraries cultivate judgment, not just deliver results
Six roles, from identity to the global alliance
Knowledge as gift, not commodity

n3.label: §3 · ROLE 01
n3.title: Identity Without Surveillance
n3.summary: The Proof of Human Library ID — a two-tier, non-surveillance credential. Each district holds a global identifier; each member a unique identifier within it. Enough to participate; never enough to track.
points: A deliberate counter to iris-scanning and biometric identity systems
Three modes: fully identified, verified-but-pseudonymous, anonymous
Custodian of the container, not the contents — keys stay with the member
1.1 billion library cards already exist (IFLA)

n4.label: §4 · ROLE 02
n4.title: Curator of Knowledge Objects
n4.summary: A new unit of stewardship for the AI age: the MISO — a knowledge object that carries its own provenance, credibility, version history, and terms of use, rather than a flat file stripped of context.
points: Library-branded AI lenses: “answer using the Arapahoe lens”
BiblioBoard / Lyrasis infrastructure already operational
Domain knowledge modules for sovereign devices
Curated corpora: 2–3 orders of magnitude more compute-efficient

n5.label: §5 · ROLE 03
n5.title: Founding the Knowledge Commons
n5.summary: Libraries as founding members of an MISO Mutual Capital Company — an Australian cooperative form that raises external capital without surrendering member control. Capital is welcomed as a guest, not a master.
points: Activating institution: Arapahoe Libraries, with Toronto Public Library
Australian partners constitutive: Trove, State Library Victoria
Six dimensions of value (Syntropic World), not one financial metric
Membership becomes citizenship in the commons

n6.label: §6 · ROLE 04
n6.title: The Homebrew AI Cohort
n6.summary: A community of practice at every library where self-sovereign AI devices are assembled, maintained, repaired, and upgraded — the Homebrew Computer Club pattern, made civic and local.
points: Right-to-repair momentum; library makerspaces already in place
Four owner-replaceable layers: LLM chip, domain modules, archive, orchestration
Kit assembly as the most concrete digital-literacy program imaginable

n7.label: §7 · ROLE 05
n7.title: Backup Steward of the Archive
n7.summary: The library as air-gapped, physical custodian of members' encrypted personal archives — steward of ciphertext, never of keys. And, when the backbone fails, the community's communications lighthouse.
points: Monthly air-gapped backup; post-quantum encryption
Split-key escrow: library, solicitor, family member
Mesh, medium-range radio, HF shortwave — graceful degradation
A fundable, mission-aligned new civic role

n8.label: §8 · ROLE 06
n8.title: The Global Library Alliance
n8.summary: A trans-oceanic alliance already in relationship — Arapahoe Library District, Denver Public Library, Toronto Library, State Library Victoria in Melbourne — sitting naturally within a middle-powers alignment of Canada, Australia, and India.
points: Universal Library District IDs as the connective tissue
Each library a complete node: seven roles in one
The Internet Archive as a natural institutional ally
Erasmus.AI and ClimateGPT as the first domain use cases

fluency.label: STEP 01 · THE FLUENCY LAYER
fluency.title: The Fluency Layer
fluency.summary: A frontier model — the “gift of the gab” — cleans an everyday question into a clear, well-formed version. Its job is to understand faithfully, never to quietly decide what you meant. Genuine ambiguity is kept, not guessed away.

reasoning.label: STEP 02 · THE REASONING ENGINE
reasoning.title: The Reasoning Engine
reasoning.summary: The part that actually reasons: it walks the trustworthy connections in the Meaning Commons and assembles a justified answer — working only with governed, accountable knowledge, never the AI's own guesswork.

finding.label: STEP 03 · THE FINDING
finding.title: The Finding
finding.summary: A complete, justified answer that carries its own evidence, its named-and-sourced connections, an honest confidence level, and any disagreements left open. Built to be handed forward — and itself a first-class MISO added to the Commons.

herald.label: STEP 04 · THE HERALD
herald.title: The Herald
herald.summary: The system's voice, delivering the Finding in plain, confident language. The locked rule: it may only say what it can trace. If it can't trace an answer to real evidence and real connections, it says so rather than bluffing.

leaps.label: THE CORE INSIGHT
leaps.title: Leaps, not facts
leaps.summary: Conclusions are stepping stones across a river: the stones are facts, the gaps are the leaps. The reasoning was never in the facts — it was always in the leaps. Every connection is treated as a statement someone is responsible for.
points: See where a conclusion is strong — and where it leans on a weak join
When experts disagree, the disagreement is held open, not smoothed away
This authority to connect is precisely what the cooperative governs

park.label: THE MORAL SPINE
park.title: Stop and park for a human
park.summary: Autonomous machine reasoning has a hard ceiling. On consequential matters where genuine disagreement remains, the system pulls over, hazard lights on, and waits for a person — and the wait has no timer. Parked means parked until someone genuinely engages.
points: Three options, set in advance: stop and ask, proceed on the record, or refuse
A deadline that lets the machine proceed is the old failure in disguise
If a use can't tolerate the wait, it shouldn't be automated at all

dv3.label: CANONICAL · VERSION 3
dv3.title: The Library Mutual & The Meaning Commons
dv3.summary: The board-level account that settles the language and governance: three names (Commons, Mutual, MISO), the “leaps not facts” insight, the Herald, and the stop-and-park spine.

dman.label: FOUNDATIONAL DOCUMENT
dman.title: The Civilizing Role of Libraries in the Ages of AI
dman.summary: The manifesto: six concrete roles libraries play in the AI age, and the invitation to library districts worldwide. Predates the v3 governance language and is due a refining pass.

dmiso.label: TECHNICAL WHITE PAPER
dmiso.title: MISO & Commons — White Paper
dmiso.summary: The full technical and philosophical account: the Acknowledgement of Country, the gift-and-interdependence Preamble, the MISO definition, attribution spectrum, crypto-native infrastructure, and the Commons registry.

dmcc.label: COMPANION DOCUMENT
dmcc.title: Mutual Capital Company
dmcc.summary: The Australian cooperative legal form that lets the commons raise growth capital without demutualisation — governance stays with members.

dsda.label: COMPANION DOCUMENT
dsda.title: Sovereign Device Architecture
dsda.summary: Personal AI devices owned, controlled, and upgraded by the individual — four owner-replaceable layers, with the personal archive sovereign and encrypted on a card the owner physically holds.
